title: calea asclepiifolia
intro: Calea asclepiifolia, also known as Mexican mint or bitter grass, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Asteraceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Calea asclepiifolia include its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, as well as its use in treating digestive issues and skin conditions. Calea asclepiifolia can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in some herbal remedies, such as salves and creams, to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
